---
title: "Resume"
---

# Clark Ruttinger

**Data & Analytics Platform Leader**  
Workforce Analytics | R/Python/SQL | Shiny/Posit Deployment | Client-Facing Data Products

Park City, Utah  
[ceruttinger@gmail.com](mailto:ceruttinger@gmail.com)  
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/clark-ruttinger/)  
[Portfolio](https://clarkruttinger.info)

# Professional Summary

Data and analytics leader with 15+ years building reproducible data products, workforce models, dashboards, and decision-support tools for government, healthcare, association, and consulting clients. Experienced translating complex business and policy questions into data pipelines, statistical models, interactive applications, and executive-facing recommendations. Strong R/Python/SQL background with hands-on experience in Shiny, Posit Connect, cloud-hosted analytics environments, data validation, documentation, and modern data architecture patterns including DuckDB and object-storage based workflows.

# Skills

- **Analytics & Modeling:** workforce supply/demand modeling, projection methods, microsimulation, survey research, program evaluation, geospatial analysis, data quality validation
- **Data Products:** R Shiny applications, dashboards, stakeholder-facing reporting tools, reusable dashboard frameworks, public analytics products
- **Programming & Data Tools:** R, Python, SQL, DuckDB, Excel, VBA, Git, Quarto/R Markdown, Shiny, Posit Connect
- **Data Engineering & Workflow:** ETL/data cleaning, reproducible workflows, dataset profiling, documentation, cloud-hosted analytics environments, direct-query architecture concepts
- **Leadership & Delivery:** client-facing consulting, executive communication, project scoping, technical translation, staff mentorship, cross-functional collaboration
- **Domains:** public-sector analytics, healthcare workforce, labor market analysis, health policy, regulated data environments

# Work Experience

## GlobalData

### Director, Health Workforce Consulting
[Team Portfolio](https://www.globaldata.com/health-economics/health-workforce/) <br>
**October 2022 -- May 2026**

- Led complex workforce analytics engagements for state agencies, hospital associations, professional societies, and federal clients, translating analytic outputs into practical planning, policy, and investment recommendations.
- Directed annual health workforce projection updates, expanded projections to new occupations, revised behavioral health projection methods, and implemented additional validation steps that reduced client revision cycles.
- Built and maintained reusable R Shiny and dashboard components, including a standalone BLS component and unified client dashboard framework deployed through Posit Connect.
- Designed a DuckDB + S3 direct-query proof of concept to replace heavier data warehouse assumptions with a simpler, lower-friction architecture for analytic workloads.
- Developed documentation for dataset profiles, data paths, staffing generation, and annual update workflows to improve repeatability, onboarding, and handoff.
- Supported technical and analytic delivery across major client projects, including Florida nursing education and workforce reports, Hawaii nursing projections, Arkansas and Tennessee workforce reports, and professional association supply/demand studies.
- Collaborated with internal consulting, HEOR, modeling, and technical teams to scope deliverables, resolve data-quality issues, and align analyses with customer decision needs.


## Utah Health Workforce Information Center

### Founding Director

**July 2022 -- October 2022**

- Helped establish Utah's legislatively created Health Workforce Information Center, translating stakeholder priorities into a statewide health workforce data and policy infrastructure.
- Engaged healthcare, education, government, and employer stakeholders to improve workforce data collection, analytics, and policy guidance under the [Utah Health Workforce Act](https://le.utah.gov/~2022/bills/static/HB0176.html).
- Served on national and state advisory groups focused on workforce data, nursing workforce strategy, hospital workforce planning, and technical assistance.
[the Health Workforce Interest Group Advisory Committee for Academy Health](https://academyhealth.org/professional-resources/interest-groups/health-workforce-interest-group/advisory-committee/page/health-workforce-interest-group-advisory-committee) 2021--2026.
[Expert work-group for the HRSA funded Health Workforce Technical Assistance Center at the University of Albany](https://www.healthworkforceta.org/about/expert-workgroup/).


## Utah Medical Education Council

### Interim Executive Director; Director, Workforce Research; Senior Analyst; Research Analyst

**January 2010 -- June 2022**

- Led research staff and managed statewide workforce research programs covering physicians, nurses, APRNs, dentists, pharmacists, PAs, and behavioral health professions.
- Designed and managed data cleaning, warehousing, modeling, survey, and visualization processes for statewide workforce supply, demand, distribution, and training pipeline analyses.
- Built cloud-hosted research infrastructure, R Shiny publication capacity, and a Neo4j graph database with a web interface for querying and analyzing state APCD data.
- Created methodology and data-linkage strategy for legislatively required nursing employment demand analysis; mentored analysts and improved reporting frequency and consistency.
- Authored and edited research reports, policy briefs, technical reports, and executive presentations used by legislators, educators, healthcare administrators, and national partners.
- Recipient of  [IBM Healthcorps grant to model Primary Care workforce based on population health needs](https://ibmhealthcorpsblog.wordpress.com)
[News article about the project](https://www.deseret.com/2018/11/4/20657880/utah-has-a-shortage-of-primary-care-physicians-this-task-force-is-teaming-up-with-ibm-to-fix-it/)
[Project presentation to Utah Legislature](https://le.utah.gov/interim/2019/pdf/00001372.pdf). 

## Earlier Public Sector and Nonprofit Roles

### State of Utah; The Road Home; Wasatch Homeless Health Care; Valley Mental Health

**2002 -- 2015**

- Built foundation in public programs, administrative hearings, grant writing, case coordination, eligibility policy, mediation, and direct service delivery.
- Used SQL interfaces, macros, and database systems to improve accuracy and efficiency in administrative and reporting workflows.

# Publications & Public Work

- **Journal of The American Physical Therapy Association:** [Current and Projected Future Supply and Demand for Physical Therapists From 2022 to 2037](https://doi.org/10.1093/ptj/pzaf014), *Physical Therapy*, 2025.
- **Florida Center for Nursing:** [Florida Healthcare Workforce Projections, 2022--2037](https://flcenterfornursing.org/research-data/nursing-workforce-reports/florida-health-care-workforce-projections-2022-2037/), Shiny dashboard for exploration of projection results. [State of Nursing Education in Florida, 2026](https://flcenterfornursing.org/research-data/nursing-education-reports/the-state-of-nursing-education-in-florida-2026-report/).
- **Hawaii Center for Nursing:** [Hawaii Nursing Projections Report](https://www.hawaiicenterfornursing.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Hawaii-Nursing-Projections-Report-v.Final-1.pdf) and [Projections Launch Deck](https://www.hawaiicenterfornursing.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Projections-Launch-Final-Deck.pdf).
- **Maryland Hospital Association**
[Maryland Nurse Workforce Projections](https://mhaonline.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/Maryland-Nurse-Workforce-Projections-GlobalData.pdf).
- **Arkansas Hospital Association**
[Arkansas Workforce Report](https://issuu.com/arkhospitals/docs/arkansasworkforcereport-january2023).
- **Tennessee Hospital Association**
[Tennessee Health Workforce Projections](https://tha.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/Tennessee-Health-Workforce-Projections-report-FINAL.pdf).
- **Journal of Rural and Remote Health:** [Bounsanga et al. Using machine learning to identify factors associated with practice location of the healthcare workforce](https://www.rrh.org.au/journal/article/7050), 2022.
- **Journal of Nursing Regulation:** [Smiley et al, The 2020 National Nursing Workforce Survey, Journal of Nursing Regulation](https://www.journalofnursingregulation.com/article/S2155-8256(21)00027-2/fulltext), 2020; [post publication dashboard of the survey data that I designed/built in R shiny](https://clarkruttinger.shinyapps.io/draftboard/) 
- **Health Workforce Research- State of Utah** <br>
[Utah Medical Education Council (2021). Utah’s Mental Health  Workforce, 2021. Salt Lake City, UT.](https://umec.utah.gov/publications/mental-health/)
[Utah Medical Education Council (2020). Utah’s Physician  Workforce, 2020. Salt Lake City, UT.](https://umec.utah.gov/wp-content/uploads/2020-Physician-Workforce-Report-final.pdf)
[Utah Medical Education Council (2020). Utah Supply of Registered Nurses and Licensed Practical Nurses, 2020. Salt Lake City, UT.](https://umec-nursing.utah.gov/wp-content/uploads/UtahRN-LPNSupply2020-Final.pdf)
[Utah Medical Education Council (2020). Utah Nursing Demand 2020. Salt Lake City, UT.](https://umec-nursing.utah.gov/wp-content/uploads/UtahRNEmploymentDemand2020.pdf)
[Utah Medical Education Council (2017). Utah’s Advanced Practice Registered Nurse Workforce,2017: A Study of the Supply and Distribution of APRNs in Utah. Salt Lake City, UT.](https://umec-nursing.utah.gov/wp-content/uploads/APRN_2017_FINAL.pdf)
[Utah Medical Education Council (2016). Utah’s Physician Workforce, 2016: A Study of the Supply and Distribution of Physicians in Utah. Salt Lake City, UT.](https://umec.utah.gov/wp-content/uploads/2016-Physicians-Report-Final.pdf)
[Utah Medical Education Council (2016). Supply of Nurses in Utah: The 2016 Survey Utah’s Registered Nurses. Salt Lake City, UT.](https://umec-nursing.utah.gov/wp-content/uploads/RN-report-final-updated5-9-2016.pdf)
[Utah Medical Education Council (2013). Utah’s Advanced Practice Registered Nurse Workforce, 2013: A Study of the Supply and Distribution of APRNs in Utah. Salt Lake City, UT.](https://umec-nursing.utah.gov/wp-content/uploads/2013APRNReport.pdf)


# Education

## University of Utah

- **Master of Business Administration**; Certificate in Information Systems, 2017
- **Master of Public Administration**, emphasis in Program Evaluation and Policy Analysis, 2007
- **B.A., Political Science**; Certificate in International Relations, 2002
- **B.A., Spanish**, 2002

# Selected Service

- **Peer Reviewer**, Journal of Rural and Remote Health, 2021--present
- **Peer Reviewer**, Human Resources for Health, 2022--present
- **Board Member and Board President**, [National Forum of State Nursing Workforce Centers](http://nursingworkforcecenters.org/), 2018--2020
- **Grant Reviewer**, U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / HRSA, 2011--2024
- **Planning Commissioner and Commission Chair**, Salt Lake City Planning Commission, 2011--2019

<!-- # Resume Downloads -->

<!-- - [Data / Analytics Platform Resume](downloads/Clark_Ruttinger_Data_Analytics_Platform_Resume.docx) -->
<!-- - [Posit Senior Customer Success Resume](downloads/Clark_Ruttinger_Posit_Senior_Customer_Success_Resume.docx) -->
